title: oxycoccus quadripetalus subsp. microphyllus
intro: Oxycoccus quadripetalus subsp. microphyllus, also known as the dwarf cranberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to parts of North America. The medicinal properties of Oxycoccus quadripetalus subsp. microphyllus lie in its rich content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help to reduce the risk of urinary tract infections and promote overall immune function. Oxycoccus quadripetalus subsp. microphyllus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional medicine to enhance its therapeutic effects.
